name: Body Fat Calculator
description: Industrial-grade body fat estimation tool using anthropometric measurements and lean manufacturing principles for health and ergonomic risk assessment.

inputs.label: Gender
inputs.businessContext: Biological sex used in anthropometric regression models (ISO 7250-1).

businessContext_i18n.en: Biological sex used in anthropometric regression models (ISO 7250-1).

inputs.label: Age
inputs.businessContext: Age affects body composition and metabolic rate (Six Sigma DMAIC phase: Measure).

businessContext_i18n.en: Age affects body composition and metabolic rate (Six Sigma DMAIC phase: Measure).

inputs.label: Height
inputs.businessContext: Stature measurement per ISO 7250-1 for normalization.

businessContext_i18n.en: Stature measurement per ISO 7250-1 for normalization.

inputs.label: Weight
inputs.businessContext: Body mass for BMI and fat mass calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Body mass for BMI and fat mass calculations.

inputs.label: Neck Circumference
inputs.businessContext: Key input for body density estimation (Navy method, Lean Six Sigma MSA).

businessContext_i18n.en: Key input for body density estimation (Navy method, Lean Six Sigma MSA).

inputs.label: Waist Circumference
inputs.businessContext: Abdominal girth for visceral fat risk (WERC ergonomic guidelines).

businessContext_i18n.en: Abdominal girth for visceral fat risk (WERC ergonomic guidelines).

inputs.label: Hip Circumference
inputs.businessContext: Required for female body fat calculation (ISO 20685).

businessContext_i18n.en: Required for female body fat calculation (ISO 20685).

inputs.label: Activity Level
inputs.businessContext: Physical activity multiplier for energy expenditure (Lean Kaizen continuous improvement).

businessContext_i18n.en: Physical activity multiplier for energy expenditure (Lean Kaizen continuous improvement).

rules.message: Waist and hip circumferences must be positive for ratio calculation.
Hip circumference is required for female body fat estimation.
Age must be between 18 and 100 years per ISO 11228-1.
Neck circumference should be less than waist circumference for valid anthropometry.

thresholds.alert: High body fat percentage for male. Consider ergonomic intervention (WERC).
High body fat percentage for female. Health risk per WHO standards.
Elevated waist circumference (>102 cm) indicates metabolic syndrome risk (Lean Six Sigma CTQ).
Elevated waist circumference (>88 cm) indicates metabolic syndrome risk.

formulas.name: Body Density (Navy Method)
formulas.description: Body density estimation using circumference measurements (Hodgdon & Beckett).
formulas.formula: gender == 'male' ? (1.0323 - 0.000154 * waistCircumference + 0.0001557 * neckCircumference - 0.000069 * height) : (1.0296 - 0.000154 * waistCircumference + 0.0001557 * neckCircumference + 0.000069 * hipCircumference - 0.000069 * height)

formulas.name: Body Fat Percentage (Siri Equation)
formulas.description: Conversion from body density to fat percentage using Siri's equation (ISO 20685).

formulas.name: Waist-to-Hip Ratio
formulas.description: Indicator of fat distribution (WERC ergonomic risk).

formulas.name: Data Confidence Adjusted Body Fat
formulas.description: Body fat percentage adjusted for measurement uncertainty (Six Sigma Gage R&R).

primary.label: Body Fat Percentage
primary.description: Estimated body fat percentage using Siri equation from body density.

hiddenLossDrivers.description: Neck circumference measurement error > 0.5 cm can bias body fat by ±2% (Six Sigma MSA).
Hydration affects body density; not accounted in Navy method (Lean waste: variation).
Self-reported activity level may overestimate energy expenditure (ISO 8996).
Siri equation assumes constant density of fat-free mass; bias increases with age (ISO 20685).

suggestedActions.description: If body fat > 25% (male) or > 32% (female): implement Lean Kaizen health improvement plan with diet and exercise.
If waist-hip ratio > 0.9 (male) or > 0.85 (female): schedule ergonomic workstation assessment per WERC guidelines.
If BMI > 30: refer to occupational health for metabolic screening (ISO 45001).
Repeat measurements monthly to reduce random error and track trend (SPC control chart).
